title: Pozole verde
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients that contribute cholesterol. The tofu, hominy, vegetables, greens, cilantro, pumpkin seeds, broth, olive oil, lime, and garnishes all contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the stew is built around plant-based ingredients including tofu, hominy, greens, chiles, and pumpkin seeds, with flavor developed through charring, blending, herbs, lime, and fresh garnishes rather than saturated fat. Its overall structure is supportive of LDL lowering.
intro: Pozole verde is a green hominy stew rooted in central and southern Mexico, where herbs, chiles, and seeds give the broth its vivid color and earthy depth. This version keeps that bright, savory identity while leaning lighter, using tofu for substance and hominy for the stew’s classic chew. It still eats like a true bowl of pozole, with charred aromatics, a smooth verde base, and a fresh finish from lime and crisp garnishes. The result is clean, lively, and satisfying without losing the dish’s regional soul.

stepGroups.title: Char the Aromatics
items: In a dry skillet or under the broiler, blister the garlic, onion, jalapeño, and poblano until softened and browned, about 5 to 7 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Blend the Verde Base
items: Add the charred vegetables, chopped greens, cilantro, cumin, pumpkin seeds, a pinch of salt, and 1/2 cup of the broth to a blender. Blend until smooth and bright green.

stepGroups.title: Simmer the Pozole
items: Pour the verde mixture into a saucepan. Add the remaining broth and the hominy. Simmer over medium-low heat for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.

stepGroups.title: Crisp the Tofu
items: Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the tofu cubes and cook until golden brown on most sides, about 7 to 8 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Soup
items: Stir the lime juice into the simmered verde broth, then gently fold in the crisped tofu.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Ladle into bowls and serve hot with avocado, red onion, shredded lettuce or cabbage, baked corn tortilla strips, lime wedges, and hot sauce.
